page.changelog.title: Updates and improvements — Persistent Cart for Shopify
page.changelog.desc: Refined continuously since 2016. Here's how we keep Persistent Cart sharp: faster silent sync, quieter cart updates, smoother setup, and reliability work.
page.changelog.h1: How Persistent Cart has improved since 2016
page.changelog.lede: Persistent Cart doesn't ship loud, numbered releases — it gets quietly better. Since 2016 we've refined the same focused job: syncing signed-in carts faster, more silently, and more reliably. Rather than a version log, here are the themes of that continuous work, so you can see how the app has matured over nearly a decade.
page.changelog.s1.h: Sync keeps getting faster and quieter
page.changelog.s1.b: The core job — moving a signed-in shopper's cart from one device to another — has been tuned repeatedly for speed and silence. The default behavior is a silent auto-merge: when a customer signs in, their saved cart combines with their current one with no popup, email, or opt-in. Over the years we've reduced the moments where a shopper sees a loader or a reload, so the experience feels less like a feature firing and more like the cart simply being where it should be. The goal has stayed constant: the shopper notices the result, not the mechanism.
page.changelog.s2.h: Small cart changes now sync silently
page.changelog.s2.b: Not every cart change is a new product. Sometimes only a line item's attributes or notes change — and those updates now sync quietly in the background, without a visible loader or a page reload. This was a deliberate refinement: small, behind-the-scenes cart differences shouldn't interrupt a shopper or make the page flicker. It's the kind of detail that never appears on a feature list, but it adds up to a calmer, more trustworthy storefront for signed-in customers moving between devices.
page.changelog.s3.b: Getting started has been simplified over time into one click and one switch: install from the Shopify App Store, then enable the app embed in your theme editor. The admin now walks you through a short setup guide so activation isn't guesswork, and that guidance has been refined to reduce the most common reason stores lose sync — forgetting to re-enable the app embed after switching or updating a theme. No theme code is edited at any point. The aim is simple: install, flip one switch, and trust that it's working.
page.changelog.s4.h: Reliability and quality work never stops
page.changelog.s4.b: Most of the work behind Persistent Cart is invisible: keeping sync accurate, safe, and fast as Shopify's platform evolves. Carts merge additively so items aren't silently dropped, shared B2B workstations stay separated so no cart leaks between accounts, and large carts with many variants are handled without slowing the page. The app meets Shopify's Built for Shopify standards for performance and security, and we keep it aligned with those standards as they change. After nearly a decade, the priority is the same as on day one: a signed-in shopper's cart should always be there, and always be theirs.
